commit_message: Fermentation monitoring is vessel-first (per-vessel modal), not a standalone round grid
diff_summary: Fermentation tracking lives ON the vessel: each vessel card on /bulk opens a "Fermentation" modal (FermentMonitor) to log sugar (Brix/Baumé), pH, and temperature over time, with a dual-Y chart (Brix left axis / Temp right axis, labeled) + pH companion, multi-row backfill (each row its own date/time), edit/remove (void + re-log; readings immutable), AF/MLF state controls, and additions. The standalone /ferment/round grid + nav entry were removed. Captures still route through the offline Dexie outbox (useSync) and reuse Phase 4 AnalysisPanel/AnalysisReading.

proposed_record.summary: Fermentation monitoring is vessel-first (per-vessel modal), not a standalone round grid
proposed_record.decision: Fermentation tracking lives ON the vessel: each vessel card on /bulk opens a "Fermentation" modal (FermentMonitor) to log sugar (Brix/Baumé), pH, and temperature over time, with a dual-Y chart (Brix left axis / Temp right axis, labeled) + pH companion, multi-row backfill (each row its own date/time), edit/remove (void + re-log; readings immutable), AF/MLF state controls, and additions. The standalone /ferment/round grid + nav entry were removed. Captures still route through the offline Dexie outbox (useSync) and reuse Phase 4 AnalysisPanel/AnalysisReading.
proposed_record.rationale: User feedback: monitoring belongs on the tank/barrel as a single place to "see it all", not a separate multi-tank page. Reusing AnalysisPanel means the readings also feed the lot's chemistry trend + the derived stuck signal.
proposed_record.revisit_conditions: If a fast multi-tank morning-round sweep is needed again, reintroduce a grid that reuses the same outbox + submitPanelCore (don't fork the capture path).

commit_message: Crush + Press merged into one /ferment/process module; "Crush" renamed "De-stem" with crusher on/off + % capture
diff_summary: De-stem and press are one module (/ferment/process, nav "De-stem & press") with a De-stem | Press tab toggle — they are the same primitive (fruit→liquid). "Crush" renamed to "De-stem" everywhere (timeline: "De-stemmed fruit → X L must"). De-stem captures a crusher-rollers On/Off toggle + "% of lot crushed" (default 100) as op metadata (crusherOn/crushedPct). Separate /ferment/crush + /ferment/press routes deleted (CrushClient/PressClient files kept, imported by ProcessClient).

proposed_record.summary: Crush + Press merged into one /ferment/process module; "Crush" renamed "De-stem" with crusher on/off + % capture
proposed_record.decision: De-stem and press are one module (/ferment/process, nav "De-stem & press") with a De-stem | Press tab toggle — they are the same primitive (fruit→liquid). "Crush" renamed to "De-stem" everywhere (timeline: "De-stemmed fruit → X L must"). De-stem captures a crusher-rollers On/Off toggle + "% of lot crushed" (default 100) as op metadata (crusherOn/crushedPct). Separate /ferment/crush + /ferment/press routes deleted (CrushClient/PressClient files kept, imported by ProcessClient).
proposed_record.rationale: User: de-stem and press are the same operation conceptually; harvest builds the fruit lot, then you de-stem OR press it. The crusher rollers can be engaged for part of a lot and off for the rest (destem whole-berry), so the % crushed must be capturable.

commit_message: Whole-cluster press (fruit→JUICE, skip crush) + additions reuse Phase 3 ops; consumed-kg ledger enforces process rules
diff_summary: Whole-cluster press presses harvest picks straight to a JUICE lot, skipping de-stem (op PRESS via crushLotCore with outputForm:JUICE/opType:PRESS; planCrushSplit splits one juice lot across N destination vessels). Press only lists MUST lots for the must-lot path. The process rules ("press once → can't de-stem after; de-stem → MUST → still pressable") need NO explicit lock — the shared LotHarvestSource consumed-kg ledger enforces them (a pressed/de-stemmed pick's remaining kg drops to 0, removing it from the other path). Additions (yeast/MLF culture/fining/tannin/SO2) are surfaced on de-stem, press, AND the monitor, reusing the Phase 3 ADDITION/FINING op (new MATERIAL_KINDS YEAST + MLF); stage-then-chain onto the new lot via applyStagedAdditions. Supply stock draw-down + cost is deferred to Phase 8 (records are Phase-8-ready).

proposed_record.summary: Whole-cluster press (fruit→JUICE, skip crush) + additions reuse Phase 3 ops; consumed-kg ledger enforces process rules
proposed_record.decision: Whole-cluster press presses harvest picks straight to a JUICE lot, skipping de-stem (op PRESS via crushLotCore with outputForm:JUICE/opType:PRESS; planCrushSplit splits one juice lot across N destination vessels). Press only lists MUST lots for the must-lot path. The process rules ("press once → can't de-stem after; de-stem → MUST → still pressable") need NO explicit lock — the shared LotHarvestSource consumed-kg ledger enforces them (a pressed/de-stemmed pick's remaining kg drops to 0, removing it from the other path). Additions (yeast/MLF culture/fining/tannin/SO2) are surfaced on de-stem, press, AND the monitor, reusing the Phase 3 ADDITION/FINING op (new MATERIAL_KINDS YEAST + MLF); stage-then-chain onto the new lot via applyStagedAdditions. Supply stock draw-down + cost is deferred to Phase 8 (records are Phase-8-ready).
proposed_record.rationale: User: fruit can go straight to press for whole-cluster (whites), skipping crush; once pressed it can't be crushed, but crushed must can still be pressed. Additions (inoculation, fining, tannin) happen at the crush pad and during ferment — they're the existing Phase 3 op, just surfaced in-context.
proposed_record.revisit_conditions: Phase 8 (supplies inventory + cost): wire these ADDITION/FINING ops to draw down stock and carry cost; turn the free-text material catalog into a stock dropdown by kind.
